headline: Forever Chemicals Bite 3M Stock Again After Australia Sues
summary: Shares of  3M  were lower in midday trading on Thursday after the Australian government sued the company for compensation over so-called forever chemicals, PFAS.  3M stock was down 2.2% at $151.75, while the  and  were up 0.6% and flat, respectively.  The dip came as the Australian government sought $1.4 billion in damages arising from the use of PFAS used in firefighting foam at defense bases.
